description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ruby:latest Docker image, with a before_script that runs `gem install bundler` and `bundle install`, and caches the vendor/ directory. Define three stages: test, deploy. Add a test job in the test stage that runs `bundle exec jekyll build -d test`, stores the `test` directory as an artifact, and runs on all branches except `master` and `work-in-progress`. Add a live-deploy job in the deploy stage that runs on the `master` branch only, executes `which ssh-agent || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install openssh-client -y )`, `which rsync || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install rsync -y )`, `eval $(ssh-agent -s)`, `ssh-add <(echo "$DEPLOY_SSH_KEY")`, `bundle exec jekyll build -d public/ --future`, and `rsync --delete -e "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" -a public/ $DEPLOY_URL`, publishes the `public` directory as an artifact, and uses tags `non-shared` and `foodsharing`. Add a pages job in the deploy stage that runs on the `work-in-progress` branch only, runs `bundle exec jekyll build -d public/ --future` and `cp public/index.de.html public/index.html`, stores `public` as an artifact, and also uses tags `non-shared` and `foodsharing`.
